records.ety: [Akin to D. rif, G. riff, Icel. rif, Dan. rev; cf. Icel. rifa rift, rent, fissure, rifa to rive, bear. Cf. Rift, Rive.]

defs: A chain or range of rocks lying at or near the surface of the water. See Coral reefs, under Coral.
A large vein of auriferous quartz; -- so called in Australia. Hence, any body of rock yielding valuable ore.

records.ety: [Akin to D. reef, G. reff, Sw. ref; cf. Icel. rif reef, rifa to basten together. Cf. Reeve, v. t., River.]

defs: That part of a sail which is taken in or let out by means of the reef points, in order to adapt the size of the sail to the force of the wind.
notes: ☞ From the head to the first reef-band, in square sails, is termed the first reef; from this to the next is the second reef; and so on. In fore-and-aft sails, which reef on the foot, the first reef is the lowest part.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Reefed (rēft); p. pr. & vb. n. Reefing.]

defs: To reduce the extent of (as a sail) by rolling or folding a certain portion of it and making it fast to the yard or spar.
